title: OrbitalJS Target Mapping
description: Schema for target-mapping.json. Defines all bodies known to OrbitalJS, their NAIF IDs, and the exact Horizons API parameters needed to fetch their ephemerides.

version.description: Semantic version of this mapping file.

targets.description: Ordered list of all fetchable bodies.

key.description: Internal stable identifier used as map key in the fetcher and as the lookup key in planets.json. Must be lowercase ASCII, no spaces.

naifId.description: Official SPICE/NAIF numeric ID. Used by your app as the canonical source-of-truth identifier. Not necessarily equal to the Horizons command string.

displayName.description: Human-readable name shown in the UI and used as the body.name in BodyState / RenderBody.

bodyType.description: Physical classification aligned with the BodyType enum in celestial-body.ts.

horizons.description: Horizons API query parameters. Kept separate from naifId because small-body identifiers follow different conventions than major-body NAIF IDs.

command.description: Value passed verbatim to the Horizons COMMAND parameter. Major bodies: numeric NAIF ID (e.g. '399'). Small bodies / asteroids: 'DES=<number>;' prefix. Comets: 'DES=<designation>;' (e.g. 'DES=1P;').

center.description: Horizons CENTER parameter. '500@10' = Sun body-center (heliocentric). '500@399' = Earth body-center (geocentric).

physics.description: Physical constants sourced from IAU / NASA Planetary Fact Sheet. Stored in SI/conventional units here; the fetcher converts to AU-day² for the RK4 engine.

gmKm3s2.description: Standard gravitational parameter GM in km³/s². The fetcher uses this to derive GM in AU³/day² for the integrators. Set to 0 for negligible-mass bodies (comets).

render.description: Visual properties used by the Canvas renderer.

color.description: CSS hex color string used to draw this body and its orbital trail.

radiusPx.description: Optional fixed display radius in pixels. When omitted, the renderer computes the radius from body mass using getBodyRadius().
